name: Cornell Dupree
abstract: Cornell Luther Dupree (December 19, 1942 – May 8, 2011) was an American jazz fusion and R&B guitarist. He worked at various times with Miles Davis, Aretha Franklin, Bill Withers, Donny Hathaway, King Curtis, and Steve Gadd, appeared on Late Night with David Letterman, and wrote a book on soul and blues guitar, Rhythm and Blues Guitar.

concerts.label: Black on Black: Up From Kansas City

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: You’ve got Cornell Dupree, who passed away. Bernard Purdie. I mean, it is serious business now... But they always present that incredible recording
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
